asymmetry: ISA contribution limits reset annually but the allowance is not cumulative — money not saved in a given year is permanently lost to the tax-free wrapper, a fact HMRC does not proactively communicate.

script.intrusion: Every pound you didn't put in an ISA this year is gone permanently.
script.weight: The annual ISA allowance is £20,000. If you don't use it by April 5th, that tax-free capacity is gone permanently — not carried forward, not recovered. HMRC does not send a reminder.
script.mechanism: HMRC created the ISA wrapper in 1999. Each April 5th, any unused allowance disappears — permanently. It does not roll over. The wrapper itself protects every pound inside it from income tax and capital gains tax for life. But HMRC does not remind you. Your bank does not remind you. The deadline is structural. The silence around it is deliberate.
script.proof: HMRC's ISA statistics, published annually, show £3.7 billion in unclaimed ISA allowance in the 2022-23 tax year. That is permanent, irrecoverable tax exposure for the people who missed the April 5th deadline.
script.comment_trigger: How much of your £20,000 ISA allowance have you used this year?
script.move: What financially-aware people use to navigate this is Trading 212.

visual_direction.frame_description: Extreme close-up of a bank statement, numbers slightly out of focus, single column of figures barely visible
visual_direction.light_source: Single overhead fluorescent tube, directly above, casting sharp downward shadows
visual_direction.motion: Ken Burns: slow pull back from 120% to 100%, revealing more of the document, 75 seconds duration
visual_direction.kling_prompt: extreme close-up bank statement paper texture, cold fluorescent overhead light, sharp downward shadows, numbers barely legible, cinematic 9:16 vertical, dark atmosphere, documentary realism, no faces, no people
visual_direction.negative_prompt: faces, people, warm colours, sunlight, lifestyle imagery, text overlays, happy, bright, colorful, outdoors

visual_dna.film: compressed video codec — slight purple fringing, real exposure decisions, no grade applied

shot_list.intent: Stop the scroll. Establish tension before explanation.
shot_list.prompt: Shot 1 (0–5s): Aesthetic: Lo-fi Authentic. real domestic environments: kitchens, bedrooms, living rooms, commutes — the ordinary world as protagonist. Empty office desk. Untouched ISA paperwork sits under a paperweight. A phone screen lights up — notification unseen. Camera: iPhone 15 Pro Max or similar consumer device, natural lens, no stabilisation, real compression. Motion: handheld human breath visible, zoom instead of dolly, accidental reframes kept. Lighting: window light at noon or lamp from wrong angle — imperfect, real, what was there. screen glow as fill — the device used to access information. Film stock: compressed video codec — slight purple fringing, real exposure decisions, no grade applied. Atmosphere: warm practical light flickers once at 2 seconds — power-grid suggestion — then stabilises. [Visual DNA — embed unchanged in every clip] Consistent emotional register and colour temperature throughout. Each shot's aesthetic serves its moment. Temporal energy consistency. Avoid: watermarks, text overlays, subtitle artifacts, camera jitter, morphing surfaces, flickering light, inconsistent shadows. Also avoid: studio, professional lighting, crisp production value, cinematic grading, cinematic lenses.

shot_list.intent: Show how it works. Documentary observation, information discovered.
shot_list.prompt: Shot 2: World: wet streets, alley reflections, neon signs in rain, anonymous urban space at 02:00 — danger aestheticised. Camera system: Cooke S4/i anamorphic primes, ARRI Alexa Mini LF — horizontal lens flare signature.  ISA wrapper mechanism shown through simple visual metaphor — money inside protected versus money outside taxed. Optics: 50mm lens, f/4.0, enough DoF to show environment and subject together. Camera: handheld controlled micro-jitter, observational documentary tracking, camera follows discovery. Lighting: neon practical: red and blue mixed from signage above, colour isolation in darkness. harsh overhead fluorescent 5600K, cold blue-white, flat institutional light, deep shadows beneath subject. Film stock: Kodak 5219 500T — heavy grain in shadows, deep blacks, colour isolation, halation on practicals. Atmosphere: steam or breath barely visible in cold air, secondary motion from ventilation barely stirs any fabric. [Visual DNA — embed unchanged in every clip] Consistent emotional register and colour temperature throughout. Each shot's aesthetic serves its moment. Temporal energy consistency. Avoid: watermarks, text overlays, subtitle artifacts, camera jitter, morphing surfaces, flickering light, inconsistent shadows. Also avoid: daylight, clean environments, cheerful colour palettes, studio lighting, suburban domesticity.
